Name of Incident: Lin Zexu Bans Opium - Prelude to the Opium War
Impact: Imperial Commissioner Lin Zexu confiscates and destroys British opium, triggering the First Opium War

Name of Incident: Hundred Days' Reform in Qing Dynasty
Impact: Emperor Guangxu's brief modernizing reform movement crushed by Empress Dowager Cixi
Important Person/Group Responsible: Emperor Guangxu, Kang Youwei, Liang Qichao

Impact: Intellectual movement advocating science, democracy, and vernacular literature over Confucian tradition
Important Person/Group Responsible: Chen Duxiu, Hu Shih, Lu Xun

Impact: Chiang Kai-shek's Northern Expedition unifies much of China; purges Communists

Name of Incident: Hans Christian Andersen - Author of Fairy Tales
Impact: Andersen's fairy tales such as The Little Mermaid and Thumbelina became world literary classics
Marx's historical materialism and communist theory transformed global political movements
Affected Population: Global working class and political movements

Impact: Engels co-authored the Communist Manifesto with Marx and supported development of Marxist theory

Name of Incident: Florence Nightingale - Founder of Modern Nursing
Impact: Nightingale's nursing reforms during the Crimean War established modern nursing practices
Mendel's pea plant experiments established the laws of inheritance and founded genetics
Michael Faraday's discovery of electromagnetic induction led to electric generators and transformers

Name of Incident: Alfred Nobel - Inventor of Dynamite and Founder of Nobel Prizes
Impact: Nobel invented dynamite and established the Nobel Prizes to recognize outstanding human achievements
Marx and Engels publish the Communist Manifesto, calling workers of the world to unite

Name of Incident: Alexander Graham Bell Invents the Telephone
Impact: Bell's telephone patent transforms communication and initiates the telecommunications era
Edison's phonograph enables audio recording and playback, founding the recorded music industry

Name of Incident: Edison Opens First Commercial Electrical Power Station
Impact: Edison's Pearl Street Station inaugurates commercial electrical power distribution
Kafka's works like The Metamorphosis and The Trial explore alienation and bureaucratic absurdity
Chanel revolutionized women's fashion by replacing corsets with comfortable and elegant designs
Pierre de Coubertin revives the Olympic Games; 14 nations participate in Athens
Important Person/Group Responsible: Pierre de Coubertin, King George I of Greece

Impact: Max Planck introduces quantum mechanics, fundamentally changing our understanding of physics
Sartre's existentialism ('existence precedes essence') influenced 20th century philosophy and literature
Fanon's works on colonialism and racism influenced independence movements across Africa and Asia
Levinas's ethics of responsibility to the Other influenced moral philosophy after the Holocaust

Name of Incident: Alan Turing - Father of Computing and Artificial Intelligence
Impact: Turing's theoretical work founded computer science; his codebreaking shortened World War II
Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR) formally established after the Russian Revolution

Name of Incident: John Logie Baird Demonstrates First Working Television
Impact: Baird's demonstration of television transmission marks the birth of the television era
Treaty of Rome formally creates the European Community, integrating European economies
ARPANET connects four universities, creating the precursor to the modern internet
Formal dissolution of the USSR ends the Cold War; 15 independent states emerge
First recorded Chinese immigrant to New Zealand; built a successful life as farmer and property developer
Affected Population: Appo Hocton and local Nelson community

Name of Incident: Chinese Miners Recruited for Otago Gold Rush
Impact: ~8,000 Chinese men from Guangdong worked the goldfields; they called NZ 'New Gold Mountain' (新金山)

Name of Incident: New Zealand Chinese Poll Tax Introduced
Impact: £10 entry fee imposed exclusively on Chinese immigrants under the Chinese Immigrants Act — one of NZ's most explicit racial discrimination laws
Poll tax increased tenfold to further restrict Chinese immigration
Formal repeal of the discriminatory poll tax on Chinese immigrants after being quietly waived in 1934

New Zealand-born Rewi Alley arrived intending a short visit and stayed 60 years; founded the Chinese Industrial Cooperative movement, ran Shandan Bailie School near the Gobi Desert, and wrote over 60 books

Name of Incident: New Zealand China Friendship Society Founded
Impact: Founded at Rewi Alley's encouragement; made NZ one of the earliest Western countries with formal people-to-people ties with the PRC

Name of Incident: New Zealand Establishes Diplomatic Relations with PRC
Impact: NZ switched recognition from the Republic of China (Taiwan) to the People's Republic of China on the same day as Australia
Important Person/Group Responsible: NZ PM Norman Kirk; Zhou Enlai

Impact: Rewi Alley died in Beijing aged 90, having spent 60 years in China; Christchurch and Gansu Province became sister cities partly as a legacy of his Shandan school

Name of Incident: New Zealand Apologises for Chinese Poll Tax
Impact: NZ became the first country in the world to formally apologise for a racially targeted immigration tax imposed on Chinese immigrants
Affected Population: Chinese New Zealanders and their descendants

Name of Incident: New Zealand First Developed Country to Back China WTO Accession
Impact: NZ completed negotiations supporting China's entry into the World Trade Organization, the first developed nation to do so
First free trade agreement China signed with any developed country; negotiations began in 2004 when PM Helen Clark and President Hu Jintao met at APEC in Santiago

Name of Incident: Xi Jinping Visits New Zealand; Comprehensive Strategic Partnership Established
Impact: China and New Zealand elevated ties to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership — one of China's highest bilateral relationship designations

Name of Incident: New Zealand Joins Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank
Impact: NZ became the first developed country to apply to join the AIIB, a China-led multilateral development bank

Name of Incident: Upgraded NZ-China Free Trade Agreement Takes Effect
Impact: Expanded FTA granted NZ access to 22 new Chinese service sectors including environmental services and air transport

Name of Incident: Oracle Bone Script - Earliest Chinese Writing
Impact: The earliest known form of Chinese writing inscribed on bones and shells; foundation of Chinese written art and calligraphy

Name of Incident: Bronze Ritual Vessels of the Shang Dynasty
Impact: Elaborate bronze vessels (ding tripods) cast for ritual use; among the finest bronze art in human history
8000+ life-sized terracotta warriors buried with Qin Shi Huang; one of the greatest sculptural achievements of the ancient world
Important Person/Group Responsible: Qin artisans under Qin Shi Huang

Name of Incident: Han Dynasty Silk Painting and Lacquerware
Impact: Exquisite silk paintings and lacquerware from Mawangdui tombs; set the standard for Chinese decorative arts

Name of Incident: Invention of Paper - Enables Chinese Calligraphy and Painting
Impact: Cai Lun's refined papermaking transformed Chinese visual arts; calligraphy and ink painting flourished as high art forms

Name of Incident: Dunhuang Mogao Caves - Buddhist Mural Art Begins
Impact: Construction of the Mogao Caves begins; over 1000 years of Buddhist murals and sculptures created along the Silk Road

Name of Incident: Tang Dynasty Poetry - Li Bai and Du Fu
Impact: Li Bai and Du Fu compose their greatest works; Tang poetry remains the pinnacle of classical Chinese literary art
Affected Population: Chinese civilization and East Asian literature

Name of Incident: Song Dynasty Landscape Painting - Fan Kuan and Guo Xi
Impact: Song masters develop monumental landscape painting (shanshui); establishes the foundational aesthetic of Chinese painting

Name of Incident: Construction of the Longmen Grottoes Completed
Impact: Carved across the Northern Wei to Tang dynasties; tens of thousands of Buddhist stone sculptures cut into limestone cliffs
Important Person/Group Responsible: Northern Wei and Tang Dynasty patrons

Name of Incident: Journey to the West Written by Wu Cheng'en
Impact: One of the Four Great Classical Novels; mythological epic blending adventure and philosophy; shaped East Asian storytelling and art

Name of Incident: Jingdezhen Porcelain - Blue and White Pottery Peak
Impact: Yuan Dynasty blue-and-white porcelain from Jingdezhen becomes the world's most coveted ceramic art; exported globally

Name of Incident: Construction of the Temple of Heaven
Impact: Built during the Yongle reign; masterpiece of Ming ritual architecture and symmetry; UNESCO World Heritage Site

Name of Incident: The Mustard Seed Garden Manual of Painting Published
Impact: Foundational illustrated manual of Chinese brush painting technique; influenced generations of painters across East Asia

Name of Incident: Qing Dynasty Cloisonné and Imperial Enamelware
Impact: Peak of cloisonné enamel artistry under the Kangxi and Qianlong emperors; imperial workshops produce works of extraordinary refinement
Vast imperial garden and palace complex; blend of southern Chinese garden design and northern palace architecture; UNESCO World Heritage Site

Name of Incident: Lu Xun Publishes A Madman's Diary - Birth of Modern Chinese Literature
Impact: First major work of modern vernacular Chinese fiction; marks the break from classical literary tradition and birth of modern Chinese literature

Name of Incident: Xu Beihong Fuses Chinese and Western Painting
Impact: Xu Beihong returns from Paris and pioneers the fusion of Western realism with Chinese ink painting; reshapes Chinese fine art education

Name of Incident: Construction of the Beijing National Stadium (Bird's Nest)
Impact: Iconic lattice steel stadium designed for the 2008 Olympics by Herzog and de Meuron with Ai Weiwei; became a symbol of modern Chinese architecture

Name of Incident: Ai Weiwei - Sunflower Seeds Installation at Tate Modern
Impact: 100 million hand-crafted porcelain sunflower seeds fill Tate Modern's Turbine Hall; landmark work of Chinese contemporary art on the world stage

Name of Incident: CCTV Headquarters Completed - Icon of 21st Century Chinese Architecture
Impact: Rem Koolhaas-designed looping skyscraper becomes the most discussed piece of contemporary architecture in China; signals China's architectural ambition
Affected Population: Chinese media and global architecture community

Impact: One of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World; pinnacle of ancient Egyptian monumental architecture
World's earliest surviving epic poem; foundational text of world literature

Name of Incident: Homer Composes the Iliad and Odyssey
Impact: Two foundational epics of Western literature that shaped Greek identity and influenced storytelling for millennia
Masterpiece of classical Greek architecture; temple dedicated to Athena and enduring symbol of Western civilization
Rock-cut Buddhist cave monuments with extraordinary murals and sculptures; UNESCO World Heritage Site
Important Person/Group Responsible: Maurya and later Vakataka Dynasty patrons

Impact: Largest amphitheatre ever built; engineering masterpiece of Roman architecture seating 80000 spectators
Dome engineering marvel of the Byzantine Empire; for nearly 1000 years the largest cathedral in the world
Important Person/Group Responsible: Emperor Justinian I; architect Isidore of Miletus

Name of Incident: Great Mosque of Córdoba Construction Begins
Impact: Masterpiece of Moorish architecture; forest of columns and horseshoe arches; symbol of Islamic art in Europe
Ornately illuminated manuscript Gospel book; considered the pinnacle of Insular art and one of Western art's greatest treasures
World's largest religious monument; supreme expression of Khmer architecture; UNESCO World Heritage Site
Iconic Gothic cathedral; pioneered the use of flying buttresses; centerpiece of French Gothic architecture

Name of Incident: Rumi - Persian Poet and Sufi Mystic
Impact: Rumi's Masnavi and Divan-e Shams are among the most widely read poetry in the world; profound influence on Islamic and world literature
Finest surviving example of Moorish palace architecture; intricate geometric tilework and muqarnas ceiling art; UNESCO World Heritage Site

Name of Incident: Giotto Paints the Arena Chapel Frescoes
Impact: Giotto's revolutionary naturalistic frescoes mark the dawn of the Renaissance; transformed European painting
Inca citadel set high in the Andes; extraordinary example of pre-Columbian architecture and urban planning; UNESCO World Heritage Site

Name of Incident: Michelangelo Completes the Sistine Chapel Ceiling
Impact: Nine scenes from Genesis painted across 500 square metres; greatest achievement of High Renaissance art
Considered the first modern novel; foundation of Western literary fiction
Grandest example of French Baroque architecture; set the standard for royal palace design across Europe
One of the most recognised works of art in the world; woodblock print defining ukiyo-e art and inspiring Western Impressionism

Name of Incident: Birth of Impressionism - Salon des Refusés
Impact: Rejected works by Manet and others shown at the Salon des Refusés; launched the Impressionist movement that transformed modern art
Wrought-iron lattice tower built as centrepiece of the 1889 World's Fair; revolutionary feat of engineering and enduring global icon

Name of Incident: Picasso Paints Les Demoiselles d'Avignon - Birth of Cubism
Impact: Radical deconstruction of pictorial space; sparked the Cubist movement and transformed 20th-century art
Unified fine art and functional design; shaped modern architecture graphic design and typography worldwide
Landmark of silent cinema and science fiction; pioneered special effects and visual storytelling in film
Dystopian novel coining terms like 'Big Brother' and 'doublethink'; most influential political novel of the 20th century

Name of Incident: One Hundred Years of Solitude Published by García Márquez
Impact: Defining work of magical realism; sparked a Latin American literary boom and won García Márquez the Nobel Prize

Name of Incident: Star Wars Released - New Era of Global Cinema
Impact: Transformed blockbuster filmmaking special effects and franchise storytelling; highest-grossing film of its era
Frank Gehry's titanium-clad deconstructivist building triggered urban regeneration and launched the 'Bilbao Effect' in architecture

Name of Incident: Louvre Abu Dhabi Designed by Jean Nouvel
Impact: Jean Nouvel's perforated dome museum creates a 'rain of light'; first universal museum in the Arab world and landmark of 21st-century architecture
Hip-hop retelling of Alexander Hamilton's life; most culturally significant Broadway production in decades; revolutionised musical theatre

Name of Incident: Choie Sew Hoy Establishes Business in Otago
Impact: Born in Guangdong, Choie Sew Hoy moved to Otago around 1868, opened a Dunedin store in 1869, supported Chinese miners, and became a pioneering figure in gold dredging — an early example of Chinese entrepreneurship shaping New Zealand's economic history.

Name of Incident: Chew Chong Contributes to New Zealand's Dairy Industry
Impact: Born in Guangzhou, Chew Chong became a major Taranaki merchant whose fungus export trade and early support for the butter industry made him a unique contributor to New Zealand's dairy heritage.

Name of Incident: Kathleen Hall Serves as Nurse and Missionary in China
Impact: New Zealand nurse and Anglican missionary Kathleen Hall went to China in the 1920s, working in hospitals and rural communities, and later provided medical support during China's war against Japan — a personal humanitarian bridge between the two countries.

Name of Incident: James Bertram Interviews Mao Zedong in Yan'an
Impact: New Zealand journalist James Bertram reported on the Xi'an Incident and became one of the few Western journalists to interview Mao Zedong in Yan'an — making him a rare New Zealand eyewitness to the defining moments of modern Chinese history.

Name of Incident: Gu Cheng — Chinese Misty Poet, Dies on Waiheke Island
Impact: Gu Cheng, one of China's most celebrated Misty Poets, lived in self-imposed exile on Waiheke Island. His death in 1993 — alongside his wife Xie Ye — became one of the most tragic episodes in modern Chinese literary history, cementing Waiheke's unexpected place in Chinese cultural memory.
Affected Population: Chinese literary world; global poetry community
Important Person/Group Responsible: Gu Cheng (顾城); Xie Ye (谢烨)

Name of Incident: Pansy Wong Becomes New Zealand's First Asian MP
Impact: Born in Shanghai, Pansy Wong was elected to the New Zealand Parliament in 1996 as the country's first member of Parliament of Asian ethnic origin — a landmark in New Zealand's multicultural political history.
Affected Population: Chinese New Zealanders; Asian community in NZ

Name of Incident: Chinese Actress Yao Chen Marries in Queenstown
Impact: Chinese actress and social media celebrity Yao Chen married in Queenstown, drawing widespread attention from Chinese audiences and becoming an organic endorsement of New Zealand as a destination for Chinese tourists and celebrities.
Affected Population: Chinese tourism market; NZ tourism industry

Name of Incident: Jay Chou Visits Wellington and Promotes New Zealand on Social Media
Impact: Taiwanese-born Mandopop superstar Jay Chou travelled in New Zealand and posted Wellington-related content to his massive Chinese-speaking fanbase, creating a high-profile modern pop-culture tourism link between Wellington and the Chinese-language entertainment world.
Affected Population: Chinese-speaking music and social media audience; NZ tourism

Name of Incident: Norman Kirk — Establishes NZ-PRC Diplomatic Relations
Impact: New Zealand Prime Minister Norman Kirk led the decision to switch recognition from the Republic of China to the People's Republic of China on 22 December 1972 — the single most important political act defining modern NZ-China relations.

Name of Incident: Bryce Harland — New Zealand's First Ambassador to China
Impact: Bryce Harland was appointed as New Zealand's first ambassador to the People's Republic of China following the 1972 recognition, institutionalising the bilateral relationship at the embassy level.

Name of Incident: Robert Muldoon — First NZ Prime Minister to Visit China and Meet Mao Zedong
Impact: Robert Muldoon made the first official visit to China by a New Zealand prime minister, meeting Chairman Mao Zedong in Beijing — a landmark in Cold War-era New Zealand foreign policy.

Name of Incident: Mao Zedong — Receives First New Zealand Prime Ministerial Visit
Impact: Mao Zedong received New Zealand Prime Minister Robert Muldoon in what was the first meeting between a NZ head of government and the founder of the People's Republic — symbolising the maturing of the post-1972 bilateral relationship.

Name of Incident: David Lange — Visits China as New Zealand Prime Minister
Impact: David Lange visited China during the mid-1980s, sustaining high-level prime ministerial contact during a period of Chinese economic reform under Deng Xiaoping and New Zealand's own nuclear-free foreign policy reorientation.

Name of Incident: Jiang Zemin — State Visit to New Zealand
Impact: Chinese President Jiang Zemin paid a state visit to New Zealand, marking one of the first Chinese head-of-state visits to the country and deepening political and economic ties.

Name of Incident: Hu Jintao — State Visit to New Zealand; Supports FTA Negotiations
Impact: Chinese President Hu Jintao visited New Zealand; the visit built the foundation for the landmark NZ-China Free Trade Agreement negotiations announced at APEC in 2004.

Name of Incident: Helen Clark — Signs Historic NZ-China Free Trade Agreement
Impact: New Zealand Prime Minister Helen Clark witnessed the signing of the NZ-China Free Trade Agreement in Beijing — the first FTA China signed with any developed country — the defining achievement of her government's China policy.

Name of Incident: Wen Jiabao — Signs NZ-China Free Trade Agreement with Helen Clark
Place Name: Beijing / Great Hall of the People
Impact: Chinese Premier Wen Jiabao joined New Zealand Prime Minister Helen Clark in signing the historic NZ-China FTA — the first free trade agreement China concluded with a developed country.

Name of Incident: John Key — Secures NZ-China Comprehensive Strategic Partnership
Impact: Prime Minister John Key hosted President Xi Jinping and together announced the elevation of the NZ-China relationship to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership — China's highest bilateral relationship tier — cementing New Zealand's place as one of China's closest Western partners.

Name of Incident: Xi Jinping — Establishes Comprehensive Strategic Partnership with New Zealand
Impact: President Xi Jinping visited New Zealand and announced the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership — one of the highest-level bilateral designations China offers — the defining upgrade in modern NZ-China relations.

Name of Incident: Li Keqiang — First Chinese Premier to Make Official Visit to New Zealand
Impact: Premier Li Keqiang visited New Zealand in 2017, becoming the first Chinese premier to make an official visit to the country and reinforcing trade and people-to-people ties during a period of record bilateral trade.

Name of Incident: Jacinda Ardern — First Official Prime Ministerial Visit to China
Impact: Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern made her first official visit to China, meeting President Xi Jinping and Premier Li Keqiang — the most significant diplomatic engagement with Beijing by a New Zealand leader in the post-FTA era.

Name of Incident: Chris Hipkins — Leads Post-COVID Trade Delegation to China
Impact: Prime Minister Chris Hipkins led a 29-strong business delegation to China, meeting Xi Jinping, Li Qiang and Zhao Leji — the most significant restoration of high-level NZ-China contact after COVID-19 disrupted bilateral relations.

Name of Incident: Li Qiang — State Visit to New Zealand
Impact: Chinese Premier Li Qiang visited New Zealand — one of the most substantial Chinese leader visits in years — covering trade, climate, and people-to-people themes, and signing bilateral cooperation documents with PM Christopher Luxon.

Name of Incident: Wang Yi — Visits New Zealand as Chinese Foreign Minister
Impact: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi visited New Zealand as part of a Pacific regional tour, meeting Deputy PM Winston Peters and briefly PM Christopher Luxon — an important step in diplomatic engagement ahead of the 2024 Li Qiang visit.

Name of Incident: Winston Peters — Hosts Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi
Impact: As New Zealand Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister, Winston Peters hosted Chinese FM Wang Yi in Wellington — his most prominent direct engagement with China's top diplomat and a signal of New Zealand's continued high-level diplomatic commitment.

Name of Incident: Christopher Luxon — First Official Visit to China as Prime Minister
Impact: Prime Minister Christopher Luxon made his first official visit to China from 17–20 June 2025, meeting President Xi Jinping and Premier Li Qiang in Beijing — the most significant NZ-China diplomatic engagement of the 2020s and a reaffirmation of the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
Important Person/Group Responsible: Christopher Luxon; Xi Jinping; Li Qiang

Impact: Māori, the indigenous people of New Zealand, arrived by canoe from eastern Polynesia and settled the islands, establishing a rich culture, language and tribal society across Aotearoa
Dutch navigator Abel Tasman became the first European to sight New Zealand; a hostile encounter at Golden Bay resulted in four Dutch sailors killed, and Tasman left without landing

Name of Incident: James Cook's First Voyage to New Zealand
Place Name: Poverty Bay / Coastline of New Zealand
Impact: Captain James Cook circumnavigated and mapped New Zealand's coastline, making first sustained contact between Māori and Europeans and opening the way for European settlement

Name of Incident: Parihaka Raid — Peaceful Māori Resistance Crushed
Impact: Government troops arrested prophet Te Whiti o Rongomai and Tohu Kākahi and demolished Parihaka village; the peaceful Māori passive resistance movement became a landmark in NZ civil rights history
Important Person/Group Responsible: NZ Government; Te Whiti o Rongomai

Name of Incident: New Zealand Becomes a Self-Governing Dominion
Impact: New Zealand achieved Dominion status within the British Empire, gaining greater political autonomy and marking a key step toward full independence
Important Person/Group Responsible: NZ Government; Prime Minister Joseph Ward

Impact: The 1918 influenza pandemic killed approximately 9,000 New Zealanders, with Māori suffering disproportionately high death rates; one of the deadliest events in NZ history
New Zealand's deadliest earthquake (7.8 magnitude) levelled Napier and Hastings, killing 256 people; the rebuilt Napier became a showcase of Art Deco architecture
The Statute of Westminster Adoption Act gave New Zealand full legislative independence from the United Kingdom, completing its transition to a fully sovereign nation
Important Person/Group Responsible: NZ Government; Prime Minister Peter Fraser

Impact: A lahar from Mount Ruapehu washed away the Tangiwai railway bridge; the Wellington-to-Auckland express plunged into the river killing 151 people — New Zealand's worst peacetime rail disaster

Name of Incident: Air New Zealand Flight 901 Erebus Disaster
Impact: An Air New Zealand sightseeing flight crashed into Mount Erebus, Antarctica, killing all 257 on board; subsequent inquiry found Air New Zealand guilty of an 'orchestrated litany of lies'

Name of Incident: Rainbow Warrior Bombing by French Agents
Impact: French intelligence agents sank the Greenpeace vessel Rainbow Warrior in Auckland Harbour, killing photographer Fernando Pereira; the first act of terrorism on New Zealand soil caused a major diplomatic crisis with France
The NZ stock market lost nearly 60% of its value following the global Black Monday crash — the worst stock market collapse in NZ history — exposing the fragility of Rogernomics deregulation

New Zealand became the first country in Asia-Pacific and the 13th in the world to legalise same-sex marriage; the bill's passage in Parliament was celebrated with a spontaneous haka in the public gallery

The Whanganui River became the first river in the world to be granted legal personhood, following 140 years of Māori struggle; the law recognised the river as an ancestor and living entity of the Whanganui Iwi

A white-supremacist terrorist killed 51 Muslim worshippers at two mosques during Friday prayers; PM Jacinda Ardern's response — banning assault weapons within weeks and launching the Christchurch Call — became a global model for crisis leadership
PM Jacinda Ardern's 'go hard and go early' COVID-19 elimination strategy made New Zealand an international model for pandemic response, achieving extended periods of zero community transmission

Name of Incident: Ernest Rutherford — Father of Nuclear Physics
Impact: Born in Nelson, New Zealand, Ernest Rutherford won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1908 and later discovered the atomic nucleus and the proton — the most significant scientific contributions by any New Zealander and among the greatest in history

Name of Incident: Edmund Hillary — First to Summit Mount Everest
Impact: New Zealand beekeeper and mountaineer Sir Edmund Hillary, alongside Sherpa Tenzing Norgay, became the first confirmed persons to reach the summit of Mount Everest — the most celebrated achievement in New Zealand's history

Name of Incident: Kate Sheppard — Leader of Women's Suffrage Movement
Impact: Kate Sheppard led the campaign that made New Zealand the first self-governing country in the world to grant women the right to vote, organising petitions signed by nearly a quarter of the adult female population
Affected Population: New Zealand women; global suffrage movement

Name of Incident: Michael Joseph Savage — Architect of New Zealand's Welfare State
Impact: As New Zealand's first Labour Prime Minister, Michael Joseph Savage introduced the Social Security Act 1938, free healthcare, and state housing — building one of the world's first comprehensive welfare states and remaining NZ's most beloved PM
Affected Population: New Zealand working class and families

Name of Incident: Richard Seddon — Longest-Serving New Zealand Prime Minister
Impact: 'King Dick' Seddon served as New Zealand's Prime Minister for 13 years (1893–1906) — the longest tenure in NZ history — presiding over women's suffrage, old-age pensions, and New Zealand's annexation of the Cook Islands

Name of Incident: Te Rauparaha — Dominant Māori Chief and Composer of Ka Mate
Impact: Ngāti Toa chief Te Rauparaha was the most powerful Māori military leader of the early 19th century; he composed the famous haka Ka Mate, now performed by the All Blacks, and played a major role in the Musket Wars
Affected Population: Māori tribes of the North and South Islands

Name of Incident: Sir Āpirana Ngata — Champion of Māori Culture and Rights
Impact: Sir Āpirana Ngata was the first Māori person to earn a university degree in New Zealand; as a parliamentarian and minister he championed Māori land rights, culture, language, and the recording of waiata — appearing today on the $50 note

Name of Incident: Katherine Mansfield — New Zealand's Greatest Literary Figure
Impact: Born in Wellington, Katherine Mansfield is considered one of the greatest masters of the short story in the English language; her works including Bliss and The Garden Party transformed 20th-century literary fiction

Name of Incident: Peter Jackson — Directs The Lord of the Rings Trilogy
Impact: New Zealand director Peter Jackson's Lord of the Rings trilogy won 17 Academy Awards including Best Picture and Best Director; it transformed New Zealand's global cultural identity and launched a major film tourism industry
Affected Population: Global cinema audience; New Zealand tourism

Name of Incident: Jonah Lomu — Rugby's First Global Superstar
Impact: All Blacks wing Jonah Lomu became rugby's first true global superstar at the 1995 Rugby World Cup, where his devastating performances against England and Scotland redefined what was physically possible in the game

Name of Incident: Richie McCaw — Most Capped and Decorated All Black
Impact: Richie McCaw captained the All Blacks to back-to-back Rugby World Cup victories in 2011 and 2015, becoming the most capped international rugby player in history and the most decorated All Black of all time

Name of Incident: Kiri Te Kanawa — New Zealand's Greatest Opera Singer
Impact: Dame Kiri Te Kanawa, of Māori descent, sang at the wedding of Prince Charles and Lady Diana Spencer in 1981 — watched by 600 million people worldwide — cementing her place as one of the greatest sopranos of the 20th century

Name of Incident: Ma Boyong Studies at University of Waikato
Impact: Chinese historical fiction author Ma Boyong (马伯庸) studied at the University of Waikato in New Zealand; he later credited his life in New Zealand as an inspiration for his writing, including the acclaimed novel 长安的荔枝 (Lychees of Chang'an) — a historical story about a Tang Dynasty official racing to deliver fresh lychees to the imperial court, widely read as a meditation on duty, bureaucracy and the individual.
Affected Population: Chinese literary world; NZ-China cultural ties

Name of Incident: Ma Boyong Publishes 长安的荔枝 (Lychees of Chang'an)
Impact: Ma Boyong's novella 长安的荔枝 became a bestseller across China; the author acknowledged that his years living in New Zealand shaped his worldview and storytelling sensibility — making the book a quiet cultural bridge between his NZ experience and Chinese historical fiction.
